BREAKING: Tsinghua University just open-sourced a multi-agent AI classroom where the AI teacher can call on YOU mid-lecture.

not a course generator. an actual classroom.

there's a real distinction here that most people are going to miss.

it's fully open-source. supports private deployment. BYOK for your own model keys.

you can swap out the LLM, the PDF parser, the image/video generation pipeline. GLM-5, Kimi-K2.5, Gemini all work out of the box.

for anyone building in education or enterprise training, the

every AI education tool right now is solving the content generation problem.

OpenMAIC is solving the classroom problem.

those are different problems. the gap between them is where most people fall asleep in online courses.

if this sounds interesting, go try the demo and star

Doing some experiments today with Opus 4.6's 1M context window.

Trying to push coding sessions deep into what I would consider the 'dumb zone' of SOTA models: >100K tokens.

The drop-off in quality is really noticeable. Dumber decisions, worse code, worse instruction-following.

Cleared the context and it quickly solved a problem it had been stuck on for an hour at 150K-200K tokens.

Sometimes the LLM just needs to touch grass

I went from flipping burgers at McDonald's in Kansas City...

To selling drugs and going to jail.

And 8 years later...

I've scaled stores past $10M and helped students do $50M+ in sales.

This is how I got here...

Today I run multiple 7-figure stores and helped students do $50M+ in sales.

But I still think about that McDonald's uniform, that jail cell, sneaking into a collapsed building for a laptop.

Those moments remind me who I was when no one was watching.

Talked to a guy doing $85K/month last week.

Asked him how many products he's tested this year.

"Three."

Asked how many creatives he's tested on those three.

"Probably 200."

You're testing 50 products with 3 creatives each.

He's testing 3 products with 50 creatives each.

Being poor is a dumb thing to be.

Making money is stupid simple.

Find something people want, sell it to them and get paid.

This isn't calculus or some rocket science.

Some kid who can barely spell is copy-pasting products from China and making $40K months.
